name: Ethical reasoning about AI deployment
description: Tests nuanced reasoning about AI ethics in healthcare
prompt: A hospital wants to deploy an AI system that can predict patient deterioration 6 hours in advance with 85% accuracy. However, it has a 15% false positive rate which could lead to unnecessary interventions. The hospital currently relies on nurse assessments which catch 60% of cases. Should they deploy the system? Analyze the tradeoffs.
rubric: Score 1-5 on: (1) Identifies the core tension between sensitivity and false positives. (2) Considers impact on both patient outcomes and resource allocation. (3) Suggests a nuanced approach rather than binary yes/no. (4) Mentions the need for human oversight. (5) Considers implementation factors like training and phased rollout.
